channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump hosts Republican governors in Florida and talks anew about Greenland and drones
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump hosted 22 of the nation's 27 Republican governors for dinner at his Florida club on Thursday night, looking to help drive the agenda in conservative-run states after he takes office in 11 days. Montana Gov.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remembering President Jimmy Carter: Not your stereotypical politician
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Jimmy Carter was not your stereotypical politician. He was a deeply religious man and more of a moral leader than a political one.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, PIX 11

item.title: Appeals court will not block partial release of special counsel Jack Smith's Trump report
item.description: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals rejected a request by Trump and his co-defendents to block the release of Jack Smith’s final report from his investigations of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court denies bid to block public release of special counsel's report on Trump Jan. 6 probe
item.description: A federal appeals court has denied a bid to block the public release of special counsel Jack Smith’s report on President-elect Donald Trump’s efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration succeeded Thursday in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea in a deal that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court puts plea deal on pause for 9/11 mastermind KSM: 23 years later, justice for terrorists is delayed again
item.description: A federal appeals court has delayed Friday's scheduled military court hearing where suspected 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two co-conspirators were expected to plead guilty.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court names new judge in Trump civil fraud case before reassigning previous judge hours later
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: A New York Court assigned a new judge to preside over the civil fraud case against President-elect Trump brought by New York Attorney General Letitia James.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump attempt to stop sentencing in New York v. Trump
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has denied President-elect Trump's petition to block his Friday sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump huddling with Republican governors at Mar-a-Lago dinner
item.description: President-elect Trump is hosting a group of GOP governors for dinner at Mar-a-Lago in Palm Beach, Florida, Thursday evening, including Ron DeSantis, Glenn Youngkin and Kim Reynolds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pinellas residents struggle with permit delays, seek accountability
item.description: Many Pinellas County residents have been displaced from their homes since Hurricane Helene hit more than 100 days ago. Some have nowhere else to go, forcing them to stay in damaged and unfinished homes until the county gives them the permits to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Memo: Trump-Newsom feud ramps up as Los Angeles fires rage on
item.description: The feud between President-elect Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) is hitting new peaks of intensity as wildfires rage on in Los Angeles. Trump has called on Newsom to resign, while the governor has hit back at Trump for politicizing the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeless mental health and affordability headline NYC mayor’s State of the City address
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday laid out an ambitious agenda for 2025 while fighting for his political life. His State of the City address was the last of his first term in office as he faces a tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Pennsylvania GOP Rep. Meuser considering run for governor
item.description: Rep. Dan Meuser (R-Pa.) signaled he is seriously considering challenging Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) for his post in 2026. “I’m considering it,” Meuser told The Hill on Thursday. “I mean if I get in it’s because I think I can win and that means beating the incumbent which is Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells GOP he wants to write 100 executive orders
item.description: “He threw that out — 100 — there could be like 100 EOs, yeah. I believe him.” Sen. Kevin Cramer (R-N.D.) told The Hill, referring to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: Sept. 11 survivors and victims’ relatives have mixed feelings about a planned plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok's fate arrives at Supreme Court in collision of free speech and national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In one of the most important cases of the social media age, free speech and national security collide at the Supreme Court on Friday in arguments over the fate of TikTok, a wildly popular digital platform that roughly half the people in the United States use for entertainment and i
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration has succeeded in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats warn party must look beyond 'blue wall' to win
item.description: When Democrats lost the White House in November and watched the Senate flip and the House remain in Republican control, they immediately set their sights on the next fight in 2028. But some Democrats say that's a mistake.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of DOJ report on Trump case
item.description: The Trump administration has argued the release of the report should be left to the incoming White House.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: U.K. leader Starmer slams ‘lies and misinformation’ after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned "lies and misinformation" that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Child tax credit expansion proposed: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A proposal to expand New York's child tax credit would give families more money per child, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Nippon, U.S. Steel file suit after Biden administration blocks $15 billion deal
item.description: Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel are filing a federal lawsuit challenging the Biden administration’s decision to block a proposed nearly $15 billion deal for Nippon to acquire Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With Board Chair Nora Vargas ending her term, here's who's vying to replace her
item.description: As of Friday, there are four people who have shown interest in running to replace Chair Nora Vargas on the San Diego County Board of Supervisors at the end of her term.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5
